Plain English Description: Rider can safely control the horse at walk and begin developing basic trot balance.
Core Accomplishments / Milestones:
• Mount and dismount safely 
• Balanced seat at walk 
• Maintain posting rhythm (short intervals) 
• Steer simple patterns 
• Identify basic tack parts
How Progress Is Evaluated: Foundational control and balance must be stable across multiple rides.
Key RiderBiomechanics (Layer 1): Rein symmetry, Upper body alignment, Core stability

Plain English Description: Rider independently manages walk and trot with structured flatwork and coordination.
Core Accomplishments / Milestones:
• Consistent posting diagonal 
• Smooth walk–trot transitions 
• Accurate 20m circle 
• Intro 2-point at trot 
• Tack and groom independently
How Progress Is Evaluated: Sustained trot control and geometry accuracy across sessions.
Key RiderBiomechanics (Layer 1): Core stability, Rein symmetry, Lower leg stability

Plain English Description: Rider introduces canter and small obstacles with balance and control.
Core Accomplishments / Milestones:
• Correct canter leads 
• Maintain canter on circle 
• Balanced 2-point 
• Ride poles & cross rails 
• Understand leads/diagonals
How Progress Is Evaluated: Advancement requires repeatable balance in more complex work.
Key RiderBiomechanics (Layer 1): Lower leg stability, Seat independence, Weight distribution

Plain English Description: Rider maintains balance in all gaits and begins influencing rhythm and line.
Core Accomplishments / Milestones:
• Consistent correct leads 
• Accurate 20m circle at canter 
• Ride 4–6 fence course (~2’) 
• Stable lower leg over fences
How Progress Is Evaluated: Promotion requires sustained stability and line control.
Key RiderBiomechanics (Layer 1): Rein steadiness, Timing of aids, Lower leg stability

Plain English Description: Rider demonstrates consistency and composure suitable for entry-level competition.
Core Accomplishments / Milestones:
• Ride 2’–2’6” course 
• Maintain consistent tempo 
• Demonstrate stride adjustability 
• Execute independent warm-up
Key RiderBiomechanics (Layer 1): Seat independence, Lower leg stability, Timing of aids

Plain English Description: Rider demonstrates technical consistency, adjustability, and horse management maturity.
Core Accomplishments / Milestones:
• Ride related distances accurately 
• Maintain consistent contact/frame 
• Jump 2’6”+ consistently 
• Demonstrate conditioning awareness
How Progress Is Evaluated: Sustained technical precision with low performance variability.
Key RiderBiomechanics (Layer 1): Half-halt coordination, Rein steadiness, Core stability
